title: viola × subglabrata
intro: Viola × subglabrata, also known as the hybrid pansy or Johnny-jump-up, is a perennial plant that is a cross between Viola tricolor and Viola × wittrockiana. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Its medicinal properties also extend to promoting relaxation, reducing stress, and improving sleep quality due to its sedative effects. In herbal preparations, Viola × subglabrata is often used to make teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used to treat various health issues, including anxiety, insomnia, and minor skin conditions.
